edge: DeAR is a framework introduced in the paper that iteratively builds a reasoning tree to tackle intricate problems within a single large language model (LLM).
DeAR adopts a tree-based question decomposition manner to plan the organization of rationales, mimicking the logical planning inherent in human cognition.
DeAR globally updates the rationales at each reasoning step through natural language feedback.
DeAR constructs more adaptive and accurate logical structures for complex problems by generating and updating the reasoning process from a more global perspective.
The approach significantly reduces logical errors across various large language models.

Reasoning Tree T is constructed with the Decompose-Analyze-Rethink cycle
The Analyze-Rethink cycle is demonstrated in Figure 2.
The target question Q is initially set as the question q0 in the root node n0.
An existing edge node nt = (qt, rt, st) is selected from T to start the cycle.
In the Decompose stage, LLMs decompose the question qt into sub-questions qt+1. In the Analyze stage, reasoning and answers are conducted for the newly generated questions.
In the Rethink stage, newly generated rationales are used to update rationales in existing upper-level nodes. The framework then returns to the Decompose stage.
The LLMs determine the reasoning tree T for Q by following the Analyze-Rethink cycle.
